properties.name: waiver:enforced_by
properties.value: Makefile AUDIT_IGNORES + .github/workflows/ci.yml (pip-audit --ignore-vuln); narrative in docs/audits/residual-risk.md (RR-1, RR-4)

properties.name: waiver:root_cause
properties.value: Python 3.9 reached EOL 2025-10-31; every advisory's first fixed release requires Python >=3.10, so none is pip-installable on this repo's 3.9 floor. No safe (3.9-installable) bump exists for any of the 19 RR-4 advisories.

properties.name: waiver:remediation
properties.value: Migrate the project to Python 3.10+ (drops 3.9). Flagged, NOT performed in this PR: it changes numpy/pandas/streamlit resolution and the ruff/mypy target and could break tests/typing/a11y — a major change to validate on its own.

properties.name: waiver:review_cadence
properties.value: Quarterly, and immediately when the Python floor moves to >=3.10.

vulnerabilities.recommendation: Upgrade to pip>=26.1 once the project's Python floor is >=3.10.

analysis.detail: RR-1 cluster. Build-time tooling only (RR-1); not a shipped runtime dependency. App never invokes pip at runtime. Installed pip==26.0.1; first fixed release is pip>=26.1, which declares Requires-Python >=3.10. This project's floor is Python 3.9 (EOL 2025-10-31), so the fix is NOT pip-installable here and forcing the pin breaks `make install`. Remediation: the Python 3.10+ migration flagged in docs/ROADMAP.md; the IDs are dropped from the audit waiver the moment the floor moves to 3.10.

vulnerabilities.recommendation: Upgrade to pillow>=12.2.0 once the project's Python floor is >=3.10.

analysis.detail: Transitive via streamlit (`app` extra); image handling in the local demo dashboard only. No untrusted image ingress. Installed pillow==11.3.0; first fixed release is pillow>=12.2.0, which declares Requires-Python >=3.10. This project's floor is Python 3.9 (EOL 2025-10-31), so the fix is NOT pip-installable here and forcing the pin breaks `make install`. Remediation: the Python 3.10+ migration flagged in docs/ROADMAP.md; the IDs are dropped from the audit waiver the moment the floor moves to 3.10.

vulnerabilities.recommendation: Upgrade to streamlit>=1.54.0 once the project's Python floor is >=3.10.

analysis.detail: `app` extra only. Local-first, single-user, demo-mode dashboard with no hosted/multi-user ingress (RR-3); not network-exposed. Installed streamlit==1.50.0; first fixed release is streamlit>=1.54.0, which declares Requires-Python >=3.10. This project's floor is Python 3.9 (EOL 2025-10-31), so the fix is NOT pip-installable here and forcing the pin breaks `make install`. Remediation: the Python 3.10+ migration flagged in docs/ROADMAP.md; the IDs are dropped from the audit waiver the moment the floor moves to 3.10.
